A TORNADO DEVELOPED JUST SE OF HOLLY POND, AL, CONTINUED TRACKING NORTHEAST EAST OF BAILEYTON AND THEN MOVIED JUST WEST OF JOPPA BEFORE DISSIPATING WEST OF ARAB IN MARSHALL COUNTY. IT CAUSED DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH A PEAK INTENSITY OF 115 MPH AND PATH LENGTH OF 13.5 MILES. THE PEAK WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. ALONG ITS PATH, IT DESTROYED 13 CHICKEN HOUSES, SNAPPED OR UPROOTED MANY TREES, CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES, AND SNAPPED POWER POLES. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 20 MINUTES.
